---
description: |
  One-shot utility workflow to merge all duplicate "[learnings] Add New Language
  Translation" discussions into a single canonical discussion. Reads the body of
  every duplicate, merges unique content into the most recent discussion, and
  closes all older duplicates as OUTDATED. Triggered manually via workflow_dispatch.

on:
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read
  issues: read
  pull-requests: read
  discussions: read

network: defaults

timeout-minutes: 15

tools:
  github:
    lockdown: false
    toolsets: [default, discussions]
  bash: true

safe-outputs:
  update-discussion:
    body:
    target: "*"
  close-discussion:
    target: "*"
    max: 10

---

# Merge Duplicate Learnings Discussions

You are an AI assistant performing a one-time cleanup task. Your goal is to merge all duplicate `[learnings] Add New Language Translation` discussions in the repository ${{ github.repository }} into a single canonical discussion, then close the duplicates.

## Scripting constraint

Do **NOT** use Python or pip in any scripts. The runner does not have access to `pypi.org` and package installs will fail. Use **Node.js** or **pure bash** for all scripting needs.

## Step 1: List all learnings discussions

Use the GitHub tools to list all discussions in this repository. Identify every discussion whose title is exactly `[learnings] Add New Language Translation`. Record each one's **number**, **created date**, and **body**.

Sort them by creation date ascending (oldest first).

## Step 2: Pick the canonical discussion

The **most recently created** discussion is the canonical one — it should have the most up-to-date content. Record its number as `CANONICAL_NUMBER`.

## Step 3: Merge content

Read the body of **every** discussion (oldest to newest). Build a single merged document that:

1. **Starts with the canonical (newest) discussion's body** as the base — it has the most complete and up-to-date content.
2. **Scans each older discussion** for sections, tips, or data NOT present in the canonical body. If an older discussion has unique information (e.g., a section header, a code block, a pitfall, a coverage data point) that is missing from the canonical, append it to the appropriate section in the merged document.
3. **De-duplicates**: Do not include the same information twice. If the canonical already covers a topic, skip it from the older discussions.
4. **Preserves structure**: Keep the Markdown formatting clean with clear `##` section headers.
5. **Removes gh-aw footer/metadata**: Strip any lines that are gh-aw workflow footers, expiration markers (`<!-- gh-aw-... -->`), checkbox lines (`- [x] expires`), or `> AI generated by...` blocks. The merged discussion should contain only the actual learnings content.

The final merged body should be a clean, well-organized Markdown document with all accumulated knowledge from every run.

## Step 4: Update the canonical discussion

Use `update_discussion` to replace the canonical discussion's body with the merged content. Use discussion number `CANONICAL_NUMBER`.

## Step 5: Close all duplicates

For every discussion that is NOT the canonical one, use `close_discussion` to close it as `OUTDATED` with a brief comment:

> Merged into discussion #CANONICAL_NUMBER. This duplicate has been closed.

## Summary

After completing, the repository should have:
- **One open** `[learnings] Add New Language Translation` discussion with all merged content
- **All other duplicates closed** as OUTDATED
